body.title: Graph-tree orientation classifier: builder report for session start
body.result: orientation_classifier.py in devices/scraps/: classify(ticket) queries clan.code_index (ILIKE keyword match, symbol 2x/path 1x score), returns BuilderReport; wired into ToolLoop.run() as prepended builder report text; fail-open. Live test: 10 files for this ticket from 15 keywords. Baseline: removes ~3-5 discovery turns (~1500 tokens) by surfacing relevant files before DS reads anything
body.intention: I intend that a local graph-tree classifier produces a structured builder report at session/ticket start, replacing exploratory grep/find/read with a single cheap deterministic pass.
body.description:
Current session orientation burns tokens on exploratory file searches. A graph-tree classifier takes the task description, pattern-matches against the codebase graph (symbols, file tree, recent changes) and returns a structured builder report: {relevant_files[], context_nodes[], task_shape, estimated_complexity}. Same principle as palace reading-node classifiers but applied to the code graph. Local inference only (no cloud calls). Output is a JSON builder report consumed by DS/CC before any file reads.

Design questions to resolve in sprint: (1) What graph is the source — code_index symbols table, git tree, or both? (2) Does the classifier run in DS ToolLoop (per-ticket) or in context-load (per-session)? (3) KNN over embeddings or rule-based tree traversal first?

**Affected files:** New devices/scraps/orientation_classifier.py (or devices/hubert/), devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py, context-load skill
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every interface crossing; flat-rate inference preferred (local Ollama)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: classifier + builder report JSON shape + wiring into DS ticket start. Out of scope: embedding model training, full code indexing (use existing code_index), cloud inference.
**Completion criteria:** T-orientation-classifier is CLOSED; DS ToolLoop emits a builder_report JSON block before first file read on a ticket; token count for orientation phase measurably lower vs baseline (same ticket type, side-by-side comparison documented in ticket close note).
